# Alauda Container Platform Billing

> Cost-management and billing documentation for the Alauda Container Platform — flexible cost models, multiple billing methods, and project- and platform-level statistics.

## Root

- [README.md](README.md): Contributor setup notes (in Chinese) for the billing-docs site: install Node.js and npm, run `yarn install`, then use `yarn dev` for the live-reload local server (left-nav changes require a restart), `yarn build` to emit static files to `dist/`, and `yarn serve` to preview the production build. Recommends VS Code with the MDX extension for authoring.

## docs

- [docs/en/apis/advanced_apis/index.mdx](docs/en/apis/advanced_apis/index.mdx): Section landing page for the Advanced APIs reference, which lists the RESTful HTTP endpoints (as opposed to Kubernetes-style APIs) exposed by cost management. Renders an auto-generated overview of child pages via the `<Overview>` component with weight 20.
- [docs/en/apis/advanced_apis/total.mdx](docs/en/apis/advanced_apis/total.mdx): Reference page for the Total cost endpoints under the `cost.alauda.io/v1beta1` API group: `/platform/cost.alauda.io/v1beta1/costs/total` for platform-wide totals and `/platform/cost.alauda.io/v1beta1/projects/{project}/costs/total` for per-project totals, rendered from OpenAPI via the `<OpenAPIPath>` component.
- [docs/en/apis/advanced_apis/usage.mdx](docs/en/apis/advanced_apis/usage.mdx): Reference page for the Usage endpoints under `cost.alauda.io/v1beta1`: `/platform/cost.alauda.io/v1beta1/costs/usage` for platform-wide resource usage and `/platform/cost.alauda.io/v1beta1/projects/{project}/costs/usage` for per-project usage, rendered via `<OpenAPIPath>` so consumers can inspect request/response schemas.
- [docs/en/apis/index.mdx](docs/en/apis/index.mdx): Top-level landing page for the API Reference section (weight 1000) that nests the Overview and Advanced APIs subsections. Uses the `<Overview>` component to auto-list child pages and sets the i18n title to "API Reference".
- [docs/en/apis/overview/index.mdx](docs/en/apis/overview/index.mdx): Overview subsection landing page (weight 10) that renders a full child-page overview via `<Overview />`, serving as the entry point to the Introduction and authentication topics that explain how to call the cost management APIs.
- [docs/en/apis/overview/intro.mdx](docs/en/apis/overview/intro.mdx): Explains that most ACP functionality is implemented through Kubernetes operators while non-K8s features such as log querying are exposed via separate RESTful Advanced APIs. Documents the shared bearer-token authentication model (`Authorization: Bearer <token>`) and walks through obtaining a token from the Web Console's Profile > API Tokens page with a `curl` example against the ACP API server.
- [docs/en/architecture.mdx](docs/en/architecture.mdx): Describes the three-component metering and billing architecture: Cost Management Server (installed in exactly one cluster, persists data in Clickhouse and configures unit prices and currency display), Cost Management Essentials (installed in the `global` cluster, exposes the query API and powers the UI), and Cost Management Agent (installed in each metered cluster, scrapes Prometheus, aggregates hourly, and ships data to the Server with retry on failure). Includes the `billing_flow.png` diagram.
- [docs/en/concept.mdx](docs/en/concept.mdx): Defines the core billing vocabulary: OpenCost (the upstream Kubernetes cost monitoring project), Cost Model (billing items, methods, unit prices, label-based pricing, cluster association), and the four supported billing methods per resource. Includes a full reference table mapping CPU/Memory (Usage, Request, Effective Usage, Project Quota) and Storage (Request Volume) to their per-hour calculation rules, with the 5-minute minimum charge and a note about StatefulSet restart accounting.
- [docs/en/fuctions/cost_model_management.mdx](docs/en/fuctions/cost_model_management.mdx): End-to-end guide to the Cost Models UI under Metering and Billing > Cost Models: viewing the model list, creating a model with required name, cluster bindings (excluding clusters already bound or lacking the agent), and a price list with default and label-differentiated prices for CPU/Memory/Storage billing methods. Documents Day-1 vs Day-2 behavior (description edits apply immediately; pricing/binding/items take effect next billing cycle), inline editing on the details page, and how to extend supported Prometheus pod labels (`kube_pod_labels`) by editing `moduleinfo.yaml` to add `additionalPodLabels` for kube-prometheus.
- [docs/en/fuctions/details.mdx](docs/en/fuctions/details.mdx): User guide for the Cost Details page (Metering and Billing > Cost Details) which queries day-granularity billing records over a custom time range up to one year, filtered by project, cluster, and namespace. Notes that only clusters with associated cost models produce data, unmounted PVs surface as `*unmounted-pvcs` records, and the table supports name search, column customization, and export.
- [docs/en/fuctions/index.mdx](docs/en/fuctions/index.mdx): Section landing page (weight 25) for the Guides section that groups the user-facing how-tos for Cost Statistics, Cost Details, Cost Model Management, and Project-Level Billing. Renders the child-page list via `<Overview>`.
- [docs/en/fuctions/project_billing.mdx](docs/en/fuctions/project_billing.mdx): Documents project-scoped billing for project administrators and developers under the Projects interface, gated by the `ait-billing` feature gate. Describes the Cost Statistics sub-page (project/namespace/cluster aggregation, monthly/weekly/daily intervals, Usages tab with Method filter) and the Cost Details sub-page (hidden project filter, permission-restricted cluster/namespace filters, search, column settings, CSV export, Hide Zero-Cost Items), with permission-based automatic data filtering throughout.
- [docs/en/fuctions/statistics.mdx](docs/en/fuctions/statistics.mdx): Walks through Metering and Billing > Cost Statistics for analyzing platform costs across dimensions: project/cluster/namespace granularity, monthly/weekly/daily intervals, optional per-billing-item breakdown (CPU/memory/storage), and a parallel Usages view. Notes the one-year query cap and that clusters without an associated cost model produce no data.
- [docs/en/howto/custom_cost_model.mdx](docs/en/howto/custom_cost_model.mdx): Step-by-step playbook for adding new billing items (worked example: GPU compute cores and GPU memory) by 1) writing PromQL usage queries using Counter/Gauge metrics with a sampling step like 5m, 2) defining a joinable label-metric query sharing a key such as `deviceuuid`, 3) creating an agent ConfigMap labeled `cpaas.io/slark.collection.config=true` in `cpaas-system` with `kind`/`category`/`item`/`period`/`labels.query`/`usage.query` fields and restarting `slark-agent` pods, and 4) creating a server ConfigMap labeled `cpaas.io/slark.display.config=true` in `kube-public` with display names, methods, divisors (e.g. 1073741824 for bytes-to-Gi), and units, then restarting `slark-server` pods.
- [docs/en/howto/gpu/index.mdx](docs/en/howto/gpu/index.mdx): Section landing page for the GPU cost model how-to guides, rendering an auto-generated overview of the child topics that walk through defining vGPU (HAMi), pGPU (NVIDIA Device Plugin), and NPU (Ascend) cost models.
- [docs/en/howto/gpu/npu_cost_model.mdx](docs/en/howto/gpu/npu_cost_model.mdx): Step-by-step guide to enabling NPU (Huawei Ascend) metering and billing on ACP v4.2+ with the Alauda Build of NPU Operator and Cost Management Agent. Walks through creating a `npu_count` PrometheusRule (from `npu_container_info`), Cost Management Agent collection ConfigMaps (`slark-agent-npu-namespace-config`, `slark-agent-project-config-npu` for `requests.huawei.com/Ascend910` project quota), Cost Management Server display ConfigMap, restarting `slark-agent`/`slark-server` pods, and associating the NPU cluster with a cost model that prices Request Usage and Project Quota in count-hours, with optional per-label pricing keyed on `modelName` (e.g. `910B4-Ascend-V1`).
- [docs/en/howto/gpu/pgpu_cost_model.mdx](docs/en/howto/gpu/pgpu_cost_model.mdx): How-to for billing physical NVIDIA GPUs (pGPU) using the Alauda Build of NVIDIA GPU Device Plugin and Cost Management Agent. Defines a `gpu_count` PrometheusRule built from `DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL`, two agent collection ConfigMaps (namespace-level via UUID and project-level via `requests.nvidia.com/gpu` quota), a server display ConfigMap mapping pGPUCount to Request Usage and Project Quota in count-hours, and pricing the resulting cost model with optional `modelName` label pricing (e.g. `Tesla P100-PCIE-16GB`, `Tesla T4`, `NVIDIA A30` obtained via `nvidia-smi`).
- [docs/en/howto/gpu/vgpu_cost_model.mdx](docs/en/howto/gpu/vgpu_cost_model.mdx): Procedure for metering and billing HAMi virtualized GPUs (vGPU) sharing heterogeneous AI devices in Kubernetes. Defines a `vgpu_core_labels` PrometheusRule joining `vGPUCorePercentage` with `DCGM_FI_DEV_SM_CLOCK` to attach `modelName`/`device` labels, agent ConfigMaps that collect vGPUCoreQuota/Used and vGPURamBytesQuota/Used per device UUID plus project-level `gpucores`/`gpumem` quotas (HAMi 2.7+), and a server display ConfigMap exposing core-hours and Gi-hours billing methods with a 1073741824 divisor. Pricing supports Request, Used, and ProjectQuota methods with label-based pricing on `modelName` and `device` (e.g. `nvidia`, `ascend`); warns that Request without GPU Overcommitment Ratio is meaningless.
- [docs/en/howto/index.mdx](docs/en/howto/index.mdx): Top-level landing page for the How-to section, rendering an Overview component that lists the GPU cost model how-to subpages.
- [docs/en/index.mdx](docs/en/index.mdx): Root navigation page for the English billing/cost-management documentation set, rendering an empty-header Overview component that links to Introduction, Installation, How-to, and Release Notes.
- [docs/en/installation.mdx](docs/en/installation.mdx): Installation guide for the three Cost Management plugins (Server, Essentials/cost-api, Agent), each documented via the Console (Marketplace > Plugin Management) and via YAML using `ModuleInfo` resources of `cluster.alauda.io/v1alpha1`. Covers downloading the plugin via `violet`, integrating the Server with ClickHouse (using the ACP Log Storage with Clickhouse plugin and `clickhouse-basic-auth` secret), selecting currency (CNY, USD, EUR, etc.), wiring cost-api and cost-agent to the Server cluster via `crossClusterDependency.costServer`, requiring ACP Monitor with Prometheus on agent clusters, and verifying installation through `kubectl get moduleinfo -l cpaas.io/module-name=...` showing `Running` status.
- [docs/en/intro.mdx](docs/en/intro.mdx): Introduction to the platform's cloud-native billing product, summarizing its purpose for fine-grained resource cost management. Lists key capabilities including Cost Model Management with per-cluster pricing strategies, four billing methods (usage / request / effective usage / project quota), platform-level Cost Statistics and Cost Details for tenant reconciliation, project-level role-based cost visibility, and label-based differentiated pricing. Documents usage limitations: dependency on the ACP Monitor with Prometheus plugin, support restricted to CPU/memory/storage resources, Kubernetes cluster compatibility caveats, and data-collection latency in measurements.
- [docs/en/release_notes.mdx](docs/en/release_notes.mdx): Release notes page for the billing product; the 4.0.0 entry currently lists no new features or enhancements and embeds a template placeholder for unfixed known issues.
